---

# UC-18.3.2 · OpenStack Neutron ML2 VXLAN Tenant Lifecycle: API and RPC Notifications for Network, Subnet, Port, Router, and Security Group CRUD with Port Binding Failure and Overlay Impact Correlation

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Change, Security, Fault, Audit &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We track when private cloud networking changes arrive in big waves or when a virtual hook-up keeps failing, so repair crews can calm tenant overlays before customer software loses its connection.*

---

## Description

Correlates **OpenStack** **Neutron** **ML2**-driven **VXLAN** (or **Geneve**) **tenant** **network** **lifecycle** signals—**network**, **subnet**, **port**, **router**, **security_group**, **floatingip**, and **router_interface** **CRUD**—across **REST** **audit** rows (**`openstack:neutron:api`**), **oslo.messaging** **notification** payloads (**`openstack:neutron:notification`**), **L3**/**DHCP**/**OVS**/**OVN** **agent** logs (**`openstack:neutron:agent`**), **syslog** fan-in (**`openstack:neutron:syslog`**), and **Keystone** **CADF** trails (**`openstack:keystone:audit`**) filtered to **networking** endpoints. Surfaces **segmentation_id**/**VNI** context, **binding:vif_type** and **binding:vif_status** stress, hypervisor-scoped **agent_dc** spreads, and **evt**/**actor** storms that precede **overlay** **blackholes**, **micro-segmentation** drift, or **silent** **east-west** loss when **ports** fail to bind during **live** **migration** or **mechanism** **driver** faults.

## Value

Cloud platform teams shorten **MTTR** when **tenant** overlays flap: **security** operations gain defensible **who**/**what**/**which** **VNI** proof for **audit** and **incident** reviews, capacity owners see **CRUD** **storms** that predict **control-plane** overload before **API** **rate-limits** trip, and **application** owners receive early warning when **binding_or_vif_fail** clusters concentrate on specific **hypervisors** rather than whole-site **underlay** faults—avoiding expensive **spine** escalations for problems that **Neutron** **RPC** backlogs or **iptables**/**OVS** flow gaps explain in minutes.

## Implementation

Land **rest_ta** (**1546**) pollers for **Neutron** **admin** JSON plus **oslo** **notification** topics on **`openstack:neutron:notification`**; mirror **API** middleware to **`openstack:neutron:api`**; ship **agent**/**daemon** logs to **`openstack:neutron:agent`** and **`openstack:neutron:syslog`**; include **`openstack:keystone:audit`** for authenticated **CRUD** attribution; normalize **VXLAN** **`segmentation_id`** and **port** **binding** fields; publish **`os_neutron_vxlan_lifecycle_core`**; maintain **`neutron_chg_suppress`** CSV for **lookup**-based maintenance windows.

## Evidence

Saved search **`os_neutron_vxlan_lifecycle_core`** scheduled every five minutes on **`index=openstack`** (or governance alias) across **`openstack:neutron:api`**, **`openstack:neutron:notification`**, **`openstack:neutron:agent`**, **`openstack:neutron:syslog`**, and **`openstack:keystone:audit`**, with Dashboard Studio panels **`tok_project`**, **`tok_resource`**, and **`binding_story`** drilldowns archived to **`audit_openstack_net`** weekly.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

A broken ML2 agent leaves multiple ports in binding_failed while RPC still emits port.update.end notifications and Neutron API logs show repeating POST /v2.0/ports retries from the same service account; Splunk rows gain bind_peak=1 with story binding_or_vif_fail across two 15-minute buckets for one project.

### Negative scenario

Heat completes a documented stack update so port CRUD counts rise with a matching chg_token row in neutron_chg_suppress whose window fully covers the bucket, no bind_problem lines appear in agent logs, and the correlation search returns zero rows after the suppressing lookup.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud with heavy forwarders able to sustain JSON HEC from OpenStack regions exceeding five thousand ports without dropping RPC notification tails—size ingest pipelines to at least 1.2× peak Neutron API RPS converted to log lines.
- Install rest_ta (Splunkbase 1546) on heavy forwarders adjacent to Neutron controllers; pin minimum 4.1.x trains compatible with your Splunk platform matrix so OAuth or basic auth stanzas stay supported.
- OpenStack releases: Yoga through Caracal tested patterns; OVN deployments should export Northbound logs separately if you need Geneve tunnel diagnostics beyond OVS agent text.
- RBAC: dedicate read-only cloud admin credentials for pollers with **`GET /v2.0/networks`**, **`GET /v2.0/ports`**, **`GET /v2.0/subnets`**, **`GET /v2.0/routers`**, **`GET /v2.0/security-groups`**, and **`GET /v2.0/floatingips`** plus CADF audit exports; never reuse Heat stack owner passwords.
- Network egress: allow HTTPS 443 from forwarders to the Neutron internal VIP and Keystone public endpoints; AMQP 5672/5671 only if a custom tap forwards RPC to Kafka first.
- NTP: controller and compute skew beyond two seconds misorders RPC versus API rows—monitor chrony alongside this UC.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **`openstack:neutron:api`** — Enable WSME or Paste audit middleware on neutron-server (often **`[oslo_middleware_audit]`** in neutron.conf) so each REST call carries **`action`**, **`initiator`**, **`target`**, **`reason`**, and latency; dedicate one HEC token per region.
(2) **`openstack:neutron:notification`** — Run an oslo.messaging notification consumer (small Python or Go service) that writes newline-delimited JSON into Splunk with sourcetype **`openstack:neutron:notification`**, subscribing to topics whose names begin with `neutron.` so `.create.end`, `.update.end`, and `.delete.end` event_type strings arrive with payload resource blocks for network, port, subnet, router, and security_group objects.
(3) **`openstack:neutron:agent`** — Collect files or journald streams from **`neutron-l3-agent`**, **`neutron-dhcp-agent`**, **`neutron-openvswitch-agent`**, and **`ovn-controller`** including binding errors and RPC timeouts; normalize multiline Python tracebacks with **`LINE_BREAKER`** in props.conf.
(4) **`openstack:neutron:syslog`** — Centralize rsyslog from controllers where neutron-server logs HTTP 502 responses, too-busy RPC queues, or database deadlocks—these often precede binding storms.
(5) **`openstack:keystone:audit`** — Land CADF events where **`target.typeURI`** references networking endpoints; use **`FIELDALIAS`** so **`initiator.id`** aligns with **`user_id`** from API logs.
(6) REST polling cadence via rest_ta: 120 seconds maximum during incidents; 300 seconds is acceptable when the notification feed is healthy.
(7) props/transforms: extract **`segmentation_id`** for tenant overlay tags; map **`binding:vif_status`** variants introduced by newer Nova and Neutron handshake fields.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Publish **`os_neutron_vxlan_lifecycle_core`** with **`earliest=-24h@m`**, **`cron`** every five minutes, and macros **`evt_storm_min`** (60), **`evt_busy_min`** (22), **`evt_watch_min`** (7), **`actor_fanout_min`** (12). Throttle alerts per **`proj`** and **`story`** pair for thirty minutes unless **`binding_or_vif_fail`** persists three consecutive windows.

```spl
(index=openstack OR index=cloud_openstack OR index=os)
(
  sourcetype="openstack:neutron:api" OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:notification"
  OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:agent" OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:syslog"
  OR sourcetype="openstack:keystone:audit")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval raw_lc=lower(_raw)
| eval evt_act=lower(trim(coalesce(action, event_action, method, http_method, cadf_action, "")))
| eval rsrc=lower(trim(coalesce(resource_type, resource, payload_type, object_type, target_typeuri_suffix, "network")))
| eval proj=trim(coalesce(project_id, tenant_id, project, initiator_project_id, os_project_id, ""))
| eval actor=trim(coalesce(user_id, user, initiator_id, ""))
| eval net_id=trim(coalesce(network_id, net_id, ""))
| eval vni=tonumber(coalesce(segmentation_id, vni, l2_vni, vxlan_vni, ""))
| eval bind_vif=lower(trim(coalesce(binding_vif_type, 'binding:vif_type', vif_type, "")))
| eval bind_stat=lower(trim(coalesce(binding_vif_status, 'binding:status', vif_status, port_status, status, "")))
| eval agent_host=trim(coalesce(agent_host, hypervisor_hostname, host, hostname, ""))
| eval is_crud=if(match(evt_act,"create|delete|update|post|put|patch") OR match(raw_lc,"(?i)\.(create|update|delete)_end"),1,0)
| eval bind_problem=if(match(bind_stat,"error|failed|inactive|down|build|unknown") OR match(raw_lc,"(?i)bindingfailed|bind.?fail|portbinding"),1,0)
| eval rsrc_norm=case(match(rsrc,"security[-_]?group"),"security_group",match(rsrc,"floating"),"floatingip",match(rsrc,"router[_]?interface"),"router_interface",match(rsrc,"router"),"router",match(rsrc,"subnet"),"subnet",match(rsrc,"port"),"port",match(rsrc,"network"),"network",1=1,rsrc)
| eval lane=case(st=="openstack:neutron:api","api_audit",st=="openstack:neutron:notification","rpc_notify",st=="openstack:neutron:agent","agent_log",st=="openstack:neutron:syslog","neutron_syslog",st=="openstack:keystone:audit","keystone_gate",1=1,"misc")
| eval ks_neutron=if(st=="openstack:keystone:audit" AND match(raw_lc,"(?i)neutron|/v2\.0/network|networking"),1,0)
| where (is_crud=1 AND rsrc_norm IN ("network","subnet","port","router","security_group","floatingip","router_interface")) OR bind_problem=1 OR (ks_neutron=1 AND match(evt_act,"post|put|patch|delete"))
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count AS evt, dc(actor) AS actor_dc, dc(agent_host) AS agent_dc, values(lane) AS lane_mix, max(bind_problem) AS bind_peak, latest(vni) AS vni_seen, latest(net_id) AS net_seen, latest(bind_vif) AS vif_seen BY _time proj rsrc_norm evt_act
| eval volume_tier=case(evt>=60 OR actor_dc>=12,"storm", evt>=22,"busy", evt>=7,"watch", null())
| eval overlay_stress=if(bind_peak>0 OR (rsrc_norm=="port" AND volume_tier IN ("storm","busy")),1,0)
| where isnotnull(volume_tier) OR overlay_stress=1
| eval story=case(overlay_stress=1 AND bind_peak>0,"binding_or_vif_fail",volume_tier=="storm","crud_storm",volume_tier=="busy","crud_spike",1=1,"tenant_churn_watch")
| lookup neutron_chg_suppress project_id AS proj OUTPUTNEW window_start window_end chg_token
| where isnull(chg_token) OR _time<window_start OR _time>window_end
| sort -evt -overlay_stress
| head 450
| table _time proj rsrc_norm evt_act evt actor_dc agent_dc lane_mix bind_peak vni_seen net_seen vif_seen volume_tier overlay_stress story chg_token
```

**Understanding this SPL** — The index union mirrors governance aliases while keeping **five sourcetypes** auditable for data-classification reviewers. **`rsrc_norm`** collapses heterogeneous resource labels into the same vocabulary Horizon uses for networks and ports. **`bind_problem`** merges structured **`binding:vif_status`** values with regex fallbacks for agent stderr when FIELDALIAS maps lag upgrades. The **`stats`** pass quantifies CRUD velocity per **`proj`** before **`volume_tier`** categorizes bursts; **`overlay_stress`** highlights port-heavy periods plus any binding failures that often precede overlay blackholes. **`lookup neutron_chg_suppress`** removes windows your change team already declared.

Tuning notes — Raise **`evt_storm_min`** when CI tenants remain noisy after allowlists; add **`| where proj!="service"`** for internal workloads. Use **`bin span=10m`** during live-migration campaigns for smoother curves.

### Step 3 — Validate
(1) Horizon UI path Project → Network → Networks: compare the segmentation ID shown for a VXLAN tenant network with **`vni_seen`** on rows sharing **`net_id`**—mismatches trace back to **`segmentation_id`** extraction.
(2) CLI: run **`openstack port show <uuid>`** for a flagged port and confirm **`binding:vif_status`** aligns with **`bind_peak`** intervals inside **`openstack:neutron:agent`** within one agent log rotation.
(3) **`timechart count by sourcetype span=1h`** over 48h: flat **`openstack:neutron:notification`** while **`openstack:neutron:api`** stays hot means the notification bridge stopped, not that tenants are idle.
(4) Compare **`actor_dc`** against the Keystone role assignment list—automation principals should dominate; unexpected human fanout deserves a ticket check.
(5) OVN-only fleets: sample **`ovn-sbctl lflow-list`** on compute when **`overlay_stress`** fires; missing flows that reference the reported VNI hints Northbound or Southbound desynchronization.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard Studio layout: Row A — timechart of **`evt`** stacked by **`story`** with **`proj`** token drilldown. Row B — accelerating table of recent **`binding_or_vif_fail`** rows showing **`vif_seen`**, **`agent_dc`**, and deep links to CMDB host records. Row C — bar chart of **`rsrc_norm`** during **`crud_storm`** so NetOps learns whether ports or routers drove the spike. Access: limit to cloud network administrators; mask raw user identifiers in non-production. Runbooks: send **`binding_or_vif_fail`** to the Neutron L2 queue with Nova compute liaison contacts; route **`crud_storm`** to platform engineering and attach Keystone CADF excerpts for identity proof.

### Step 5 — Alert design, visualization tuning, and troubleshooting
Alerts: trigger P2 when **`story`** equals **`binding_or_vif_fail`** for two consecutive fifteen-minute buckets; trigger P3 when **`volume_tier`** equals **`storm`** without an active **`chg_token`**. Dedup keys: concat **`proj`**, **`story`**, and date_hour. Include **`lane_mix`** in the alert payload so responders see whether **`api_audit`**, **`rpc_notify`**, or **`agent_log`** dominated.

Visualization: cap simultaneous **`proj`** series at twelve by sorting on total **`evt`**; color **`binding_or_vif_fail`** crimson, **`crud_storm`** amber, **`crud_spike`** teal, and **`tenant_churn_watch`** slate. Add a single-value panel contrasting binding failures versus benign churn.

Troubleshooting: zero results after Horizon edits means HEC dead-letter queues, wrong **`props.conf`** sourcetype stamping, or search heads lacking index access—run **`metadata type=sourcetype index=openstack`** for all five lanes. Null **`proj`** on syslog lines requires **`FIELDALIAS`** from neutron-server request IDs or API joins via **`transaction`**. Widespread HTTP 429 on pollers demands longer rest_ta intervals and cached admin tokens. Notifications without API audits usually means audit middleware was disabled during an upgrade—re-enable **`oslo_middleware_audit`**. Binding problems without agent files signals forwarders missing compute paths—audit **`inputs.conf`** whitelists. Sudden **`actor_dc`** inflation from one application credential is normal for large batches—pivot on **`actor`** before paging hardware teams. After Caracal upgrades, expect RPC volume swings of roughly forty to sixty percent until you rebaseline macros.

## SPL

```spl
(index=openstack OR index=cloud_openstack OR index=os)
(
  sourcetype="openstack:neutron:api" OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:notification"
  OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:agent" OR sourcetype="openstack:neutron:syslog"
  OR sourcetype="openstack:keystone:audit")
earliest=-24h@m
| eval st=lower(sourcetype)
| eval raw_lc=lower(_raw)
| eval evt_act=lower(trim(coalesce(action, event_action, method, http_method, cadf_action, "")))
| eval rsrc=lower(trim(coalesce(resource_type, resource, payload_type, object_type, target_typeuri_suffix, "network")))
| eval proj=trim(coalesce(project_id, tenant_id, project, initiator_project_id, os_project_id, ""))
| eval actor=trim(coalesce(user_id, user, initiator_id, ""))
| eval net_id=trim(coalesce(network_id, net_id, ""))
| eval vni=tonumber(coalesce(segmentation_id, vni, l2_vni, vxlan_vni, ""))
| eval bind_vif=lower(trim(coalesce(binding_vif_type, 'binding:vif_type', vif_type, "")))
| eval bind_stat=lower(trim(coalesce(binding_vif_status, 'binding:status', vif_status, port_status, status, "")))
| eval agent_host=trim(coalesce(agent_host, hypervisor_hostname, host, hostname, ""))
| eval is_crud=if(match(evt_act,"create|delete|update|post|put|patch") OR match(raw_lc,"(?i)\.(create|update|delete)_end"),1,0)
| eval bind_problem=if(match(bind_stat,"error|failed|inactive|down|build|unknown") OR match(raw_lc,"(?i)bindingfailed|bind.?fail|portbinding"),1,0)
| eval rsrc_norm=case(match(rsrc,"security[-_]?group"),"security_group",match(rsrc,"floating"),"floatingip",match(rsrc,"router[_]?interface"),"router_interface",match(rsrc,"router"),"router",match(rsrc,"subnet"),"subnet",match(rsrc,"port"),"port",match(rsrc,"network"),"network",1=1,rsrc)
| eval lane=case(st=="openstack:neutron:api","api_audit",st=="openstack:neutron:notification","rpc_notify",st=="openstack:neutron:agent","agent_log",st=="openstack:neutron:syslog","neutron_syslog",st=="openstack:keystone:audit","keystone_gate",1=1,"misc")
| eval ks_neutron=if(st=="openstack:keystone:audit" AND match(raw_lc,"(?i)neutron|/v2\.0/network|networking"),1,0)
| where (is_crud=1 AND rsrc_norm IN ("network","subnet","port","router","security_group","floatingip","router_interface")) OR bind_problem=1 OR (ks_neutron=1 AND match(evt_act,"post|put|patch|delete"))
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count AS evt, dc(actor) AS actor_dc, dc(agent_host) AS agent_dc, values(lane) AS lane_mix, max(bind_problem) AS bind_peak, latest(vni) AS vni_seen, latest(net_id) AS net_seen, latest(bind_vif) AS vif_seen BY _time proj rsrc_norm evt_act
| eval volume_tier=case(evt>=60 OR actor_dc>=12,"storm", evt>=22,"busy", evt>=7,"watch", null())
| eval overlay_stress=if(bind_peak>0 OR (rsrc_norm=="port" AND volume_tier IN ("storm","busy")),1,0)
| where isnotnull(volume_tier) OR overlay_stress=1
| eval story=case(overlay_stress=1 AND bind_peak>0,"binding_or_vif_fail",volume_tier=="storm","crud_storm",volume_tier=="busy","crud_spike",1=1,"tenant_churn_watch")
| lookup neutron_chg_suppress project_id AS proj OUTPUTNEW window_start window_end chg_token
| where isnull(chg_token) OR _time<window_start OR _time>window_end
| sort -evt -overlay_stress
| head 450
| table _time proj rsrc_norm evt_act evt actor_dc agent_dc lane_mix bind_peak vni_seen net_seen vif_seen volume_tier overlay_stress story chg_token
```

## Visualization

(1) **Timechart** — **`evt`** stacked by **`story`** per **`proj`**. (2) **Heatmap** — **`rsrc_norm`** × **`lane_mix`** with **`bind_peak`** overlay. (3) **Table** — top **`port`** rows with **`vif_seen`**, **`agent_dc`**, drill to **agent** logs. (4) **Single value** — count of **`binding_or_vif_fail`** versus **`crud_storm`**. (5) **Timeline** — **RPC** **`rpc_notify`** versus **API** **`api_audit`** latency offsets for change audits.

## Known False Positives

• **heat_stack_network_refresh** — Heat or Terraform stack updates replay many subnet and port create/delete calls per minute while VXLAN segmentation identifiers are reassigned on purpose. Correlate the burst window with stack identifiers you record in neutron_chg_suppress (chg_token, window_start, window_end) so crud_storm rows are not paged as incidents.

• **ansible_tenant_network_play** — Ansible Tower or AWX roles that rebuild tenant networks may flood security group and rule updates from a single automation principal. Require actor to match known automation accounts and fail open when proj appears on ansible_project_allow.csv via lookup.

• **octavia_member_rebalance** — Octavia load-balancer member reconciliations churn Neutron port rows during amphora failover or pool edits without a customer misconfiguration. Suppress binding_or_vif_fail when change records cite Octavia maintenance and vif_seen briefly toggles between ovs and unbound on the same port.

• **neutron_quota_reset_burst** — Quota reset APIs or project migration runbooks issue paired floating IP and router interface churn. Confirm timestamps against cloud administrator change tickets and carry neutron_quota_reset_burst labels in your exception register before treating the pattern as abuse.

• **dhcp_agent_failover_drill** — DHCP agent active or standby exercises restart namespaces and can emit binding errors that resemble compute faults. Escalate binding_or_vif_fail only when multiple agents or syslog RPC timeout strings appear, not during a single-node drill tagged in dhcp_agent_failover_drill with lookup filters.

• **ml2_rollout_rebind_wave** — Mechanism driver migrations from OVS to OVN or ML2 driver upgrades force fleet-wide port rebinds. Book ml2_rollout_rebind_wave maintenance hashes inside neutron_chg_suppress for TripleO or Kolla-Ansible cutovers so overlay_stress does not fire on expected rebind noise.

• **tempest_network_gate_spike** — Tempest and other CI tenants hammer the networking APIs nightly. Drop tempest-shaped project_id values using openstack_ci_tenant_allow.csv or equivalent proj allowlists keyed off actor naming conventions.

• **manual_cluster_heal_port_resync** — Operators running neutron-ovs-cleanup or ovn-nbctl sync sweeps can mark ports down transiently. Cross-check controller syslog for heal language and require manual_cluster_heal_port_resync in the change ticket before paging executives for apparent binding loss.
